finding: Long animation frame entries expose duration, blockingDuration, firstUIEventTimestamp, and script attribution for main-thread congestion. Browser support must be checked explicitly, and unsupported instrumentation cannot be interpreted as a clean performance result.
design_implication: Mirror Cartographer interaction evidence should use a fail-closed budget verdict over retained long-animation-frame entries, with an explicit unsupported classification, rather than relying on visual smoothness or average frame rate alone.

verification.source_inspection: committed module and test were re-read through the GitHub contents API
verification.test_execution: not executed in this run because no repository checkout or workflow runner was available

uncertainty: The specification is an editor's draft and may change. Browser implementation coverage is not assumed.
trust_limit: A passing verdict applies only to the exact retained observation window and interaction sequence. It does not prove universal smoothness, device-wide responsiveness, or causal attribution.
falsification_route: Run the committed tests in a clean checkout, then retain buffered and live long-animation-frame entries during the same exact target interaction. Reject the performance claim when support is absent or any declared budget is exceeded.
